author: Dr. Manuel Reyes
content:
URGENT CARE FOLLOW-UP — 1/26/2026, 11:00 AM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Interpreter: Spanish-language phone interpreter (Language Line interpreter #44218).

INTERVAL HISTORY:
2-week follow-up after empiric initiation of hydroxychloroquine and levothyroxine on 1/12/2026. Patient reports she has been taking HCQ 200 mg BID consistently since the visit. Joint pain is unchanged but no worse — she still has prolonged morning stiffness and some difficulty with grip activities. Hair shedding is unchanged. She has not started an exercise routine. No new symptoms — denies fever, rash, oral ulcers, chest pain, dyspnea, dysuria, bowel changes. No medication side effects: denies nausea, headache, vision changes, rash from HCQ; no palpitations or weight changes from levothyroxine.

She has called two of the sliding-scale rheumatology clinics from the list provided. Hopkins is offering an initial visit in approximately 10-12 weeks (mid-April) pending completion of a financial-aid application. Esperanza Health has scheduled her for a primary care intake on 2/10/2026 — they will help her with the rheumatology referral and additional lab orders.

She was unable to afford the send-out lab panel last week. Today the labs were drawn here as part of this visit (sliding-scale arrangement extended).

VITALS:
BP 114/72, HR 88, RR 14, T 98.2 F, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 50.6 kg (slight decrease from 50.8). BMI 22.5.

FOCUSED EXAM:
MSK: Persistent symmetric synovitis of MCPs (2-5) and PIPs (2-4) of both hands. Wrists tender bilaterally with decreased flexion. Ankles minimally swollen, less tender than 2 weeks ago. Grip strength still reduced bilaterally but reports it is no worse.
Skin: Diffuse scalp hair thinning unchanged. No new rash. No photosensitive distribution. No nail changes.
General: No new findings.

LAB RESULTS (returned today from initial send-out + drawn today):
From 1/12/2026 send-out (now resulted):
- Anti-dsDNA: negative (<10 IU/mL)
- Anti-Smith: negative
- Anti-Ro/La (SSA/SSB): negative
- Complement C3: 102 mg/dL (normal 90-180)
- Complement C4: 24 mg/dL (normal 10-40)
- Anti-CCP: negative (<7 U/mL)
- Hepatitis B sAg: negative; Hepatitis B core Ab: negative
- Hepatitis C Ab: negative
- HIV 1/2 Ag/Ab: non-reactive
- Parvovirus B19 IgM: negative; IgG: positive (consistent with past infection, not active)
- Free T4: 1.0 ng/dL (normal 0.8-1.8)
- TPO antibody: 312 IU/mL (POSITIVE — normal <34) — consistent with autoimmune thyroiditis (Hashimoto's)
- Urinalysis with microscopy: negative for protein, blood, casts; pH 6.0, SG 1.018; protein:creatinine ratio 0.04 (normal <0.2)

From today's draw:
- Repeat CBC: WBC 4.62, Hgb 12.4, Plt 268 (stable)
- Repeat BMP: Na 139, K 4.1, Cl 104, CO2 23, BUN 10, Cr 0.4, Glu 87 (stable)

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

22-year-old woman tolerating HCQ and levothyroxine without obvious adverse effect after 2 weeks. Specific connective tissue serologies returned uniformly negative (anti-dsDNA, anti-Sm, anti-Ro/La, anti-CCP); complements are normal. Hepatitis B/C and HIV negative. Parvovirus B19 IgM negative (no active infection). TPO antibody is positive, consistent with autoimmune thyroiditis underlying her elevated TSH. Urinalysis is bland.

The negative specific lupus serologies do NOT rule out evolving SLE — clinical disease can predate specific autoantibody development by months to years — but they do reduce the immediate specificity of the working diagnosis. Her current presentation may best be characterized as undifferentiated connective tissue disease (UCTD) with concurrent Hashimoto's thyroiditis. Other CTDs (early RA, MCTD, Sjogren's) remain on the differential but are less supported by today's results.

We will continue HCQ and levothyroxine until rheumatology can evaluate. Plan to recheck TSH in 6-8 weeks from levothyroxine initiation.

PLAN:
1. Continue hydroxychloroquine 200 mg PO BID; counseled about ocular toxicity again and need for baseline ophthalmology exam (still pending — no appointment yet).
2. Continue levothyroxine 25 mcg PO daily, fasting. Recheck TSH and free T4 in 4-6 weeks (target around 3/15/2026).
3. Continue ibuprofen 400 mg PRN for joint pain; counseled about NSAID risks.
4. Esperanza Health PCP intake 2/10/2026 — written instructions in Spanish provided to bring records.
5. Hopkins rheumatology — financial aid application begun by mother today; will follow up.
6. Patient counseled to return for new rash, oral ulcers, fever, chest pain, foamy/dark urine, leg/face swelling, eye pain, or worsening joint or fatigue.
7. Today's visit time: 35 minutes (including interpreter time 22 minutes).
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Manuel Reyes, MD
Date: 1/26/2026
